skill.name: route
skill.description: Manually route a query to the optimal Claude model (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus)
skill.summary: Manually route a query to the optimal Claude model (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus)
skill.author: 0xrdan

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, model names, and validation text as shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance. Manual review found no executable code, network access, filesystem access, secret access, prompt injection attempt, or malicious behavior in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Spans
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to Markdown code fences and inline code examples for /route usage. These lines document command syntax and do not execute Ruby, shell commands, or user-controlled code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown usage examples and tables. No executable script, interpreter directive, or shell invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings correspond to descriptive text about Claude model routing, not cryptographic functions. No hashing, encryption, signing, or security algorithm implementation appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural-language skill metadata and an example prompt. There is no cryptographic API usage or algorithm selection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings map to validation and error-handling instructions for checking the requested model name. The text does not inspect system state, enumerate files, query users, or collect host information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe input validation and usage help. No host or environment reconnaissance behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Automatic model selection can choose a model that does not match your cost, speed, or depth needs. This skill lets you manually route a prompt to Haiku, Sonnet, or Opus through simple command arguments.
actual_capabilities: Parses a /route command with a model argument and a user query.
Accepts haiku, fast, sonnet, standard, opus, and deep as routing aliases.
Maps each supported alias to a named executor subagent.
Routes simple tasks to Haiku, typical coding tasks to Sonnet, and complex analysis to Opus.
Returns usage guidance when the model argument is missing or unsupported.
limitations: It depends on compatible subagents named fast-executor, standard-executor, and deep-executor.
It does not create or configure the target subagents by itself.
It only supports the model aliases listed in the skill instructions.
It does not evaluate whether the chosen model is the best fit for the query.

use_cases.title: Control Cost for Routine Work
use_cases.description: Route quick fixes, syntax questions, and small edits to Haiku when speed and cost matter most.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Model Choices
use_cases.description: Give teammates a clear command for choosing the intended Claude model before starting a task.

use_cases.title: Escalate Complex Reviews
use_cases.description: Send architecture analysis, security review, or deep debugging prompts to Opus on demand.

prompt_templates.title: Ask a Fast Question
prompt_templates.scenario: Use the fastest route for a simple development question.
prompt_templates.prompt: /route haiku What is the syntax for a TypeScript interface?

prompt_templates.title: Run a Standard Coding Task
prompt_templates.scenario: Use the default balanced model for typical implementation work.
prompt_templates.prompt: /route sonnet Refactor this validation function and explain the tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Perform Deep Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Use the most capable route for complex reasoning.
prompt_templates.prompt: /route opus Analyze this authentication flow for security and reliability risks.

prompt_templates.title: Use Alias-Based Routing
prompt_templates.scenario: Use short aliases when speed matters during repeated work.
prompt_templates.prompt: /route deep Compare these database migration options and recommend a rollout plan.

output_examples.output: The request is routed to the fast executor using Haiku. The response focuses on a concise summary.

output_examples.input: /route standard Fix the failing unit test.
output_examples.output: The request is routed to the standard executor using Sonnet. The response provides a practical debugging path.

output_examples.input: /route deep Review this payment flow for edge cases.
output_examples.output: The request is routed to the deep executor using Opus. The response emphasizes detailed analysis and risk review.

best_practices: Use Haiku or fast for short, low-risk tasks where cost matters.
Use Sonnet or standard for everyday coding, debugging, and documentation work.
Use Opus or deep for complex analysis, architecture, and security-sensitive questions.
anti_patterns: Do not route every task to Opus when a smaller model is enough.
Do not use Haiku for work that needs deep multi-step reasoning.
Do not rely on this skill unless the required executor subagents are available.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It routes a prompt to a specific Claude model by parsing the requested model alias and query.

faq.question: Which models can I choose?
faq.answer: You can choose Haiku, Sonnet, or Opus using the aliases defined in the skill instructions.

faq.question: Does it choose the best model automatically?
faq.answer: No. It gives the user manual control over model selection.

faq.question: Does it run shell commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill only contains prompt instructions and Markdown examples.

faq.question: What happens if I enter an invalid model?
faq.answer: The skill instructs the assistant to show usage help with the supported model names.

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: The report marks the skill as compatible with Claude, Claude Code, and Codex.
